title: Fix seccomp support
body:
If user does not specify seccomp file or seccomp file does not exist,
then use the default seccomp settings.

Still need to not hard code /etc/crio/seccomp.json, should move this to
/usr/share/seccomp/seccomp.json

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: We don't do this for SELinux or any other security feature... Do we need to do it for Seccomp? I'm comfortable with saying that podman and CRI-O are responsible for adding it the the spec they pass to us

I am fine with putting the constants back into podman if this is what you prefer.  But I really want these shared between all of the tools.  So we end up with the constants defined in three places.  SELinux policy defines the defaults for it, so we already have a shared constant between the three packages.

We need a good story for sharing config between podman and CRI-O. I really want to rip out the lingering CRI-O config parsing in podman, it's causing us no end of pain, but that will break our ability to share configs, including this.

Right I see libpod as the shared config story between then and then move most of the shared config to the /usr/share/containers and /etc/containers directories.  We already do this for storage items and image items.
/etc/containers/registries.conf 

ls /etc/containers/
policy.json  registries.conf  registries.d  storage.conf

Then allow each tool to override if they want.

Alright, in that case it would make sense to put Seccomp config in libpod. We will need a way to disable adding the default seccomp config to a container (I imagine Privileged would do that too, but we'd probably want a NoSeccomp option too for just Seccomp).

We should also start thinking of making a libpod config file that would hold things like Seccomp default configs.

Looking at it more, I think it's OK that we keep the constants in libpod, though I don't like sticking them in oci.go - can you move them over to runtime.go for now?

We might even want to make a specific package for constants. There are some from CRI-O we use right now (a few annotations) that are duplicated between us and the CRI-O repo
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 8745eaa) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@mheon I think I have fixed the segfault and added better debug statements to the test suite to make something like this easier to diagnose.
PTAL
I think we need a way of easily getting a test-only runtime - no backing DB, no networking, maybe no containers/storage. That can probably happen in a separate PR
